objectives: By the end of the week, students will be able to solve problems involving the division of fractions by using diagrams, writing equations, and reasoning about the relationship between multiplication and division. They will represent problems involving fractional divisors to answer questions like "How many groups?" and "How much in one group?".
agenda: Delta Math Warm-Up > Notebook & Pencil > Launch Question
Lesson 6: Using Diagrams to Find the Number of Groups
Lesson 7: What Fraction of a Group?
Lesson 8: How Much in Each Group? (Part 1)
Lesson 9: How Much in Each Group? (Part 2) (Optional)
languageObjectives: Students will justify their reasoning about division and tape diagrams orally and in writing.
Students will explain how to create and make sense of division diagrams.
Students will use precise terminology—including whole, equal-size, fraction of, and unknown—to interpret and represent division situations.

objectives: By the end of the week, students will be able to describe the relationship between the size of the divisor and the size of the quotient in division problems. They will represent division situations using equations like $a \div b = \frac{a}{b}$ and diagrams to answer two fundamental questions: "How many groups?" and "How much in each group?".
agenda: Delta Math Warm-Up > Notebook & Pencil > Launch Question
Lesson 1: Size of Divisor and Size of Quotient
languageObjectives: Students will use the terms dividend, divisor, and quotient.
Students will interpret division situations by creating tape diagrams.
Students will justify their reasoning about division.
